notes: The autopilot's work queue: experiments to run, priority-ordered. The planner picks the next
runnable one given the current corpus. kind: sweep (find a knee across a swept dimension), ab
(two matched arms), grade (single-run verdict / regression sentinel). gatedOn=track-b means the
experiment needs live boot to inject its controlled X-axis (flag/pin) and self-generate matched
replicates; grade-kind experiments run TODAY against any matching RPT. Each names the metric it
keys on and the scenario recipe it drives (see scenarios.json).

experiments.question: At what unit count does server FPS collapse, and does it move with HC count?
experiments.why: highest-leverage open perf question; replaces the ~450-470 folklore with a measured knee per HC config

experiments.question: Does a 2nd headless client raise server FPS at matched population, or just relocate load?
experiments.why: decides whether 2-HC is worth it; matched-pop A/B is the clean test

experiments.question: Does GDIR_LEDGER hold/grow (alive) vs decay to zero (inert), and does GDIR_ORDER fire?
experiments.why: regression sentinel for PR #815; runnable now against any a-life RPT

experiments.question: Does an AI commander ever field and fly air within the window, or is Aircraft-Factory capture the blocker?
experiments.why: owner research interest (planes/choppers flying); needs the test-verb to force the precondition

experiments.question: What is the run-to-run variance of serverFpsMedian at a fixed regime?
experiments.why: must be measured FIRST: it calibrates every A/B's significance threshold and every golden band. Run before any A/B is trusted.

experiments.question: Is there a cheap 'world feels alive' win from ambient skirmishes without denting server FPS?
experiments.why: world A-life interest; A/B flag-off vs flag-on at matched regime
